title: Food Service Managers
rationale: Food service managers handle complex operational tasks requiring human judgment in menu planning, staff management, and customer experience. While generative AI can assist with scheduling, inventory analysis, and training documentation, the interpersonal and real-time decision-making aspects remain challenging for current LLM technologies.

title: Registered Nurses
rationale: Nursing requires complex human interaction, physical assessment, and nuanced clinical judgment that generative AI cannot fully replicate. While documentation and administrative tasks may be partially automated, direct patient care remains highly dependent on human empathy and tactile skills.

title: Software Developers
rationale: Software development faces high generative AI disruption through code generation tools like GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT, which can auto-generate code snippets, complete functions, and provide architectural suggestions. While complex system design still requires human judgment, routine coding tasks and junior developer work are increasingly automatable.

title: Architecture Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Architecture teaching involves complex design critique and conceptual instruction, but generative AI tools like DALL-E, Midjourney, and specialized architectural design AIs are rapidly transforming design visualization, precedent analysis, and conceptual sketching. LLMs can now generate design proposals, critique architectural concepts, and provide research summaries that will significantly augment and partially replace traditional teaching methods.

title: Computer Programmers
rationale: Generative AI tools like GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT are already capable of generating significant code segments, automating routine programming tasks, and providing intelligent code completion. Philippine programmers will likely see substantial workflow transformation, with AI handling basic coding tasks while humans focus on complex system design and architectural challenges.

title: Computer Systems Engineers/Architects
rationale: Computer Systems Engineers/Architects engage in complex design and integration tasks where generative AI can significantly augment architectural planning, code generation, and systems optimization. LLMs like GitHub Copilot and specialized AI design tools are increasingly capable of producing sophisticated infrastructure and architectural blueprints.

title: Law Teachers, Postsecondary
rationale: Law teachers face significant AI disruption through legal research automation, case analysis generation, and curriculum content synthesis. While complex legal reasoning remains challenging for LLMs, routine lecture preparation, research summaries, and academic writing can be substantially augmented or partially automated.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Security Workers
rationale: First-line security supervisors require physical presence, situational judgment, and context-specific decision-making that current generative AI cannot reliably replicate. While administrative documentation and reporting might be AI-assisted, core supervisory tasks remain human-dependent.

title: Bookkeeping, Accounting, and Auditing Clerks
rationale: Bookkeeping and accounting clerks perform highly routinized numerical processing and documentation tasks that generative AI can increasingly automate through advanced spreadsheet integration, transaction classification, and financial report generation. Philippine financial services are rapidly digitalizing, accelerating LLM adoption in standardized accounting workflows.

title: Correspondence Clerks
rationale: Correspondence clerks perform highly routinized text-based tasks that generative AI can effectively replicate, including drafting standardized responses, analyzing information requests, and generating contextually appropriate communication across multiple communication channels.

title: Carpenters
rationale: Carpentry involves physical fabrication, precise spatial reasoning, and on-site adaptation that current generative AI cannot replicate. While design tools might assist planning, actual construction requires manual skills and real-world spatial problem-solving.

title: Electricians
rationale: Electricians require significant physical on-site technical skills and spatial reasoning that generative AI cannot easily replicate. While AI might assist with documentation, wiring diagrams, and code compliance checking, the core work involves hands-on electrical installation and troubleshooting that demands direct manual intervention.

title: Delivery riders
rationale: Delivery riders primarily perform physical transportation tasks with limited generative AI applicability. While routing and dispatch are increasingly algorithmic, the core physical labor of navigating and delivering goods remains human-dependent.

title: Driver/Sales Workers
rationale: Driver/sales workers involve physical transportation and direct customer interaction, which limits pure LLM automation. However, route optimization, order tracking, and administrative tasks could be partially automated by generative AI tools.

title: Heavy and Tractor-Trailer Truck Drivers
rationale: Truck driving involves complex spatial navigation and physical vehicle operation that current generative AI cannot replace. However, route optimization, documentation, and logistics planning could be significantly augmented by AI systems.
